regulationSummary.overview: Louisiana has no gold deposits and is not a gold prospecting destination. The state is entirely underlain by Cenozoic sedimentary deposits — thick sequences of clay, silt, sand, and salt domes built by Mississippi River and Gulf Coast processes. There is no gold-bearing basement rock anywhere in Louisiana. The state's mineral resources are dominated by salt, sulfur, oil, and natural gas. This entry is included for completeness.
keyRules: Louisiana does not have a recreational gold prospecting permit system — there are no gold deposits in the state.
In-stream work in Louisiana waterways is regulated by the Louisiana Department of Natural Resources and Army Corps of Engineers.
Fossil and mineral collecting on Louisiana state land may require authorization — contact the Louisiana Department of Wildlife and Fisheries for current rules.
regulationSummary.hpaInfo: Not applicable — there are no gold-bearing streams in Louisiana.
regulationSummary.suctionDredgeStatus: Not applicable — Louisiana has no productive placer gold streams.
regulationSummary.handPanning: Gold panning in Louisiana waterways is not a viable activity — the state has no gold-bearing geology. Louisiana's alluvial rivers (Mississippi, Red, Atchafalaya) contain sediment derived from vast sedimentary formations with no gold content.
regulationSummary.disclaimer: Regulations change. Verify current rules at the official sources listed below before going out.

officialResources.title: Louisiana Geological Survey
officialResources.description: State geological survey — Louisiana has no gold-bearing geology; the survey focuses on oil, gas, salt, and coastal geology.

officialResources.title: Louisiana Department of Natural Resources
officialResources.description: State agency managing Louisiana's mineral and natural resources, primarily oil, gas, and water.
